text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Chapter Background — p.70.

Chapter 2 — Seven Days to the Grave. Korvosa quiets after the riots — and then Queen Ileosa's true horror begins: a manufactured plague, Blood Veil, loosed to cull the citizens she despises. The PCs (now ~L4) race to find a cure as the city dies around them.

The conspiracy (GM only). Ileosa always meant her husband's murder to be only the start. Through the Red Mantis assassin Kayltanya she obtained Vorel's phage; the Mantis connected her to the 'gentleman's killer' Dr. Reiner Davaulus, who with the Urgathoan zealot Lady Andaisin and the vampire-scholar Ramoska Arkminos weaponized the phage into the fast, dramatic, terror-spreading blood veil.

The delivery. A fortune in Korvosan silver was tainted with blood veil using a death's head coffer and scattered through the poor quarters and the Bank of Abadar — striking the very priests best placed to fight it. As cover, the conspirators sank the plague-ship Direption in the Jeggare as a red herring: investigators who chase the ship waste days while the tainted coin spreads. Letting blood veil run its course cripples the city's will to resist Ileosa's deeper pacts.

This is the payoff of the Blood Veil setup from Chapter 1. Run the persistent-plague overlay from here (Conversion Guide journal → Blood Veil & the Epidemic Clock): start the Epidemic Clock now, seeded by how the city fared in 'Edge of Anarchy.'

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Advancement Track / Part 1: Infection — p.70.

Advancement. The PCs begin well into 4th level → 5th after their first plague mission → 6th before Carowyn Manor / Racker's Alley → 7th before the Temple of Urgathoa beneath the hospice → just 8th by the chapter's end.

The seven days. Blood veil takes ~7 days to kill, so the early deaths are isolated among the poor and go unnoticed for days. Keep the PCs busy first (the most time-consuming early task is smuggling Trinia Sabor out of the city), and give them time to resupply and form attachments — the coming tragedies should land personally.

The Second Harrowing. Zellara's deck performs its second reading at the chapter's start, generating this chapter's Harrow Points (Conversion Guide → The Harrowing). This chapter is tied to the suit of shields and to Constitution — read the cards grim: mass graves, ashen-faced women (the Gray Maidens), carrion birds (the Queen's Physicians).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, NPC Developments — p.71.

Threads from Chapter 1 continue even where the NPCs have no direct role here:

  • Amin Jalento barricades himself in his Old Korvosa home, survives the plague, and resurfaces in Chapter 3.
  • Devargo Barvasi locks down Eel's End to ride out the quarantine — but unless the PCs intervened, his ettercap @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.chittersnap00001]{Chittersnap}, starving, turns on and kills him (his silk-wrapped body found in the hold). If Chittersnap died in Chapter 1, Devargo survives and Eel's End can be a safe haven.
  • Neolandus Kalepopolis, the missing seneschal hiding in Old Korvosa, is captured by the Arkonas as this chapter opens — the hook into Chapter 3.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Cressida's Concern — p.72.

Within hours of Trinia's botched execution, Field Marshal Cressida Kroft seeks out the PCs, worried. Queen Ileosa is restructuring Korvosa's military; Cressida fears for her own position and warns the PCs that her ability to fund their work — and to shield them — is shrinking.

Mechanically: the PCs should no longer consider themselves official Korvosan Guard agents. Cressida still aids them where she can and asks where to reach them — establishing her as their fraying but loyal patron as the city tilts toward martial law.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Rise of the Gray Maidens — p.73.

Sabina Merrin first appeared in gleaming red-and-crimson full plate at Trinia's near-execution. She now commands the Gray Maidens — an all-women elite loyal only to Ileosa, handed the Longacre Building (arbiter Zenobia Zenderholm welcomes them; the bound efreeti Yzahnum and the bloatmage seneschal Togomor speed their arming) as their headquarters.

The truth. Recruits are chosen for beauty and strength; those who fail are disfigured and imprisoned in the dungeons below. Those who pass endure a breaking, scarring initiation. Present them at first as saviors of order — the personification of martial law — and let their brutal nature surface across the chapter. Once they appear, Ileosa retreats into the sealed Castle until Chapter 3.

Use @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenFoot01]{Gray Maiden Foot Soldier} for rank-and-file Gray Maidens in this and later chapters.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, A Damsel in Distress — p.76.

Vencarlo Orisini (secretly Blackjack, who rescued Trinia from the gallows) sends the PCs a cryptic note inviting them to his Old Korvosa academy at 16 Hillcrest Street.

My friends, I hope this note finds you in good health in these dangerous times. In light of the recent unrest, I feel that I could provide some tips on self-defense to adventurers such as you, and would be most gratified to meet with you for these lessons, offered gratis, at Orisini Academy, 16 Hillcrest Street. I eagerly await your arrival. Vencarlo Orisini.

Inside, he reveals @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.triniaSabor00001]{Trinia Sabor} in disguise ('Sorry about the trouble I gave you in the Shingles,' she offers, smiling) and makes his pitch standing:

'You were all at the queen's debacle, so I don't doubt you recognize this charming young woman. I had only just reached my home the night of Her Majesty's morbid gala when that troublemaker Blackjack and this startled woman arrived at my doorstep. The so-called 'people's hero' and I have had some dealings in the past, but it's been some years since I've seen the scoundrel. He was quick with his words and soon swooped off, doubtlessly to right some other festering wrong, but not before entrusting Miss Sabor to my protection and care.

Although I don't know Blackjack's motives or politics, I trust his judgment and have seen much right done by his blade. He says Miss Sabor is innocent of the crime she's been accused of, and I'm more disposed to trust a hero of the city than the tantrums of some bloody-minded child playing at queen.

'The matter is simple: Korvosa is no longer safe for Miss Sabor. I've arranged for friends in Harse-a couple of wellrespected ranchers-to take in our beautiful renegade until this whole 'assassination' foolishness blows over. It's the first leg of the journey where we find our problem, though. Ever since the queen's put a price on her head, mercenaries, soldiers, and the queen's new Gray Maidens have been searching for the young lady tirelessly.

They've stopped by here three times so far, and each time I've only just barely been able to turn them away without inviting a search. My most reliable contacts have gone to ground in light of the recent uprisings, and Her Highness's bounty for Trinia's capture makes the use of new agents inadvisable. Thus, after some time to let her trail cool, I turned to you resourceful lot. Care to escort a lady home?'

He asks the PCs to smuggle her to safety — to a wayside inn called Trots (12 miles north), where Jasan Adriel takes her on to a ranch in Harse. He funds the trip (a horse, supplies, 20 gp for the inn) and, if pressed, offers 500 gp. He won't come himself — too well known, too outspoken against the crown — and plans to vanish into Old Korvosa for a time: don't look for him; he'll call when the time is right.

A subtle egress is best — a low-key walk out the gates among the afternoon merchants. Crimson-tunic'd guards, Hellknights, mercenaries, and the odd Gray Maiden eye the party, but pass unless the PCs do something suspicious (showy magic, a fight). Optional hinterland encounters en route let Trinia (a bard) fight beside them toward 5th level.

GM: Ileosa has set a 5,000 gp bounty on Trinia. Trinia strongly suspects Vencarlo is Blackjack but keeps it to herself; she warms to the PCs and may become a friend (she returns in Chapter 4).

Rewards: deliver Trinia to Trots → 2,400 XP. Befriended, she gifts the party her masterwork mithral dagger and her wand of daze monster ('I won't need them in Harse — you might').

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Yellow Lights — p.79.

One night early in the chapter (ideally while the PCs are escorting Trinia), the queen's red herring sails in: the Direption, a black-sailed Urgathoan ship, slips up the Jeggare just before midnight hung with yellow lanterns at the figurehead. The Wall of Eodred's trebuchets fire again and again, waking all of North Point; across the river, Trail's End watches the sleek brig sink into the dark water. Come morning the Crimson Throne says nothing, and ghost-ship gossip drowns the facts.

Asking around (Diplomacy to Gather Information) turns up rumors by result:

CheckRumor
@Check[type:diplomacy|dc:10]The Guard destroyed a ship of Riddleport pirates sneaking in by night. (False.)
@Check[type:diplomacy|dc:15]A sinister ship refused inspection and made no answer near North Bridge, so the watch fired on it. (True.)
@Check[type:diplomacy|dc:20]No guard who hailed the ship got any response — some say nobody was aboard at all. (Mostly true.)

A @Check[type:society|dc:21] (or Sailing Lore) identifies a single yellow bow-light as the Inner Sea signal for a ship under quarantine.

GM: downplay the wreck for now — it should feel like a handled danger, background flavor. As the plague spreads, rumors that the mystery ship was its source circulate on their own, and the wreck becomes Mission 1. If the PCs insist on diving it immediately, run the wreck without Yvicca (the cult hasn't moved her in yet).

pages.name: The First Symptoms & A Second Favor

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, The First Symptoms / A Second Favor — p.80.

Days before the Direption sank, young Brienna 'Breeze' Soldado of Trail's End found a bulging pouch of 50 silver coins in an alley — the biggest fortune of her life — thanked Desna, and spent it all in a day. The coins were tainted by the cult's death's head coffer. By nightfall she was failing; after a sleepless night her mother Tayce sent for an Abadaran cleric and for her late husband's brother, Grau.

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grauSoldado00001]{Grau Soldado} — shaved, sober, and back with the Guard, but visibly distressed — tracks the PCs down and pulls them somewhere private:

'My niece is sick. I don't know what she has and neither does anyone in Trail's End. She's broken out all over in red pocks and can barely keep down food, or even the swill that goodfor-nothing herbalist gave her. Her mother's talking about going to the Bank of Abadar, but can't afford to pay the prices their clerics would demand. Then I remembered how you and your friends handled yourselves during the riots, and how you aided me, and I figured you could help. A bunch of resourceful folk like you, I'd bet if you don't already have a way to fix this, you must know who can. Surely you can't just sit by while a child suffers, can you?'

He can offer only 100 gp (50 short of a remove disease casting), and resorts to guilt if refused. At the squat Soldado home the boys play quietly downstairs while ragged coughing fills the loft; the Abadaran acolyte @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ishaniDhatri0001]{Ishani Dhatri} brews cinnamon-and-anise herbs in the kitchen he can't quite make work — he doesn't recognize Brienna's combination of symptoms, and fears a new disease. (@Check[type:perception|dc:21] overhears Grau upstairs scolding Tayce over the healer's bill.)

The creaky steps open up into a bedroom loft above the main room of the Soldado home. A young girl lies in one of the beds, her slight frame dwarfed by the bed's size and the pile of pillows, afghans, and quilts surrounding her. Splotches of an angry red rash cover her face and arms, appearing in irregular shapes and sizes. Suddenly, her restlessness is interrupted by a violent fit of hacking coughs that jerk her entire frame, lifting her well off her pillows. The spasm passes after a moment, dropping her back to the bed, but seemingly having done little to ease her breathing.

Saving Brienna: she has Blood Veil (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained} from Con loss) and dies within days untreated. Remove disease cures it; lesser restoration staves off the drain. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ishaniDhatri0001]{Ishani} — out of spells today — suggests buying a remove disease potion from his temple, or for 150 gp he returns to cast it next morning. @Check[type:perception|dc:21] reads how ashamed he is to ask; @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:17] persuades him to cast it free (he spends the rest of the day praying Abadar's forgiveness).

Exposure. Treating Brienna exposes the PCs and Ishani to Blood Veil — each attempts @Check[type:fortitude|dc:24] or contracts it (the staged disease in the Conversion Guide). This is the plague's first on-screen case: start the Epidemic Clock now (Conversion Guide → Blood Veil & the Epidemic Clock).

The tainted-coins clue. Cured, Brienna asks if lunch is ready — and goes evasive if pressed about how she got sick. She secretly fears Desna punished her for not sharing the coins; @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:28] (or detect thoughts) coaxes the fear out, and the harder the PCs push, the more protective Tayce gets. The PCs aren't meant to get the coin lead this early — and the taint has already worn off the scattered silver, so scouring the city for coins now is a time-eating goose chase. Don't let it derail them; do let it pay off as foreshadowing when the death's head coffers surface (G14).

Story award: cure Brienna → 1,600 XP. Tayce cooks the PCs a feast and her door is always open; @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ishaniDhatri0001]{Ishani} — impressed — asks if he may call on them for charitable work and can be reached at the Grand Vault of Abadar; befriended, he heals them free thereafter.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Signs of the Plague (sidebar) — p.82.

From here to the chapter's end, Korvosa sinks under the cloak of pestilence. If the PCs need more ways to fight (or witness) the spread, drop in these two sketched events — as color, or expanded into full side-missions:

  • Burn Up the Dead. The city burns its dead in mass graves in the Gray District; plumes of rancid black smoke become a fixture of the southern skyline. Then one mass grave opens into a ghoul warren, loosing angry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.LHHgGSs0ELCR4CYK]{ghouls} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.VC7rtYR4hLxwg7WZ]{ghasts} into the graveyard district — at your option their bites carry blood veil instead of ghoul fever.
  • The Merciless Way Massacre. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenFoot01]{Gray Maidens} enforcing a block-scale quarantine square off against a terrified mob that won't comply. If nobody talks the crowd down, the standoff becomes a riot and the Maidens cut down nearly 100 citizens. Diplomacy beats steel here — give the PCs the chance to defuse it (and feed the result into the Survivor Count).

Part 2: Outbreak. If the PCs do nothing, roughly 5,000 Korvosans die before blood veil burns out. From here, most encounters end with a Survivor Count — track lives saved and lost; the running total drives the Epidemic Clock overlay and the chapter-end rewards (→ Final Survivor Count).

pages.name: The Unwashed Masses & the Missing Arbiter

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, The Unwashed Masses / The Missing Arbiter — p.82.

Ishani leads the PCs to the Grand Vault of Abadar (North Point), seat of Archbanker Darb Tuttle and the city's most powerful faith — and, by Ileosa's design, the institution best placed to fight the plague and so the one her tainted silver strikes first: several acolytes, guards, and vaultkeepers have already sickened, the clergy's healing magic sold out to a few wealthy worshipers within hours (fewer than two dozen citizens cured in all).

Towering over the surrounding buildings, the Grand Vault of Abadar offers a radiant vision of divine luxuriance amid a sea of mortal troubles. As its gray-veined white marble reflects the midday sun, there's little question that this place is the house of a god.

Yet for a deity of law, the steep stairs and ramps leading up to the temple's great bronze doors offer a strangely discordant scene. Dozens of citizens-mostly of the working class, although the silks of a few merchants show through the crowd-throng the entry, scarcely being held back by a group of gold-armored Abadaran clerics. All seem intent on gaining entry to the temple, but the clerics turn away nearly all comers. The clerics' reasoning becomes clear as one desperate believer is turned away, his pitiful countenance mottled with violent red sores.

To reach the doors the PCs must shove through the desperate crowd — @Check[type:athletics|dc:15] each. Any obvious healer is mobbed for aid, and actually healing (or any show of divine power) in the mob's sight nearly sets off a riot; the watching Korvosan Guard forgives thrown punches but arrests weapon- or spell-violence, even in defense. Ishani's name (or his company) gets the PCs past the door guards.

Story award: navigate the crowd without harming anyone → 1,600 XP.

The Missing Arbiter (GM only — a Chapter 4 hook). The church frets over its most prominent missing worshiper: senior arbiter Zenobia Zenderholm, who handed the Longacre Building to the Gray Maidens and vanished. She soon resurfaces proclaiming allegiance to Queen Ileosa, symbol of Abadar gone, all church contact rebuffed — Archbanker Tuttle writes her off as a 'doubting soul lost to her fears.' The truth: Zenobia died of blood veil among its earliest victims; her dying prayers went to Urgathoa, who raised her as a penanggalen. She now masquerades as a living turncoat. The PCs learn her true fate in Chapter 4 ('A History of Ashes', Part 3) — plant the wrongness now, resolve nothing.

The Healer's Hands. Inside the besieged, fear-silent hall, Ishani draws the PCs into a western meeting room and lays out what he knows:

'Thank you for your aid. I assume you already suspect my reasons for calling, having seen the crowd outside.' He shakes his head sadly. 'Poor lot. You recognize the symptoms too, I'm sure. I had hoped that the Soldado case was isolated, but apparently we have a bigger problem on our hands than I'd feared.

'Most of the patients we're treating have come from North Point and Old Korvosa. The disease seems to spread fastest through the lower classes. Although we here at the temple can heal some of the ill, I dread that the transmission of the disease will soon outpace our resources. The only way to stem the growing infection is to involve the entire city. We need to organize. We need to call upon the faiths of Sarenrae, Pharasma, and even Asmodeus to face this attack.

Archbanker Tuttle and several of his assistants are out pursuing alliances with these other faiths, but even that won't be enough. We need to involve the Korvosan Guard and the queen's new agents, the Gray Maidens, at the very least. That's where you come in. With the number of desperate souls growing, it's not particularly safe for a priest to walk the streets of Korvosa. I hear you have a good relationship with Field Marshal Cressida Kroft. Perhaps you would be willing to escort me to Citadel Volshyenek for an introduction?'

Escort pay: the church has cleared 200 gp per PC for the errand (Ishani adds 50 gp apiece from his own purse if they hesitate). What he knows beyond the briefing: the first outside cases were a barkeep and his wife from the Three Rings Tavern, cured and sent home — then three more within the hour, and more every hour since.

From here the chapter opens into First, Do No Harm, then the cure missions (the Direption, the sewers, Lavender, Racker's Alley, Carowyn Manor) and finally the Hospice of the Blessed Maiden / Temple of Urgathoa. Advance the Epidemic Clock as in-city days pass.

pages.name: First, Do No Harm & the Queen's Physicians

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, First, Do No Harm — p.85.

Reaching Citadel Volshyenek is easy — the streets conduct business in whispers, as if noise might draw the plague's notice. Hours before the PCs arrive, Kroft received royal orders: the Guard must escort and obey Queen Ileosa's new Queen's Physicians and treat any Gray Maiden's command as a superior officer's. The introduction is in full swing as the PCs enter:

The echoes of forcefully spoken but unintelligible words resound off the imposing granite and iron walls of Citadel Volshyenek's outer curtain. Dozens of red-and-silverarmored guards stand in assembly upon the pitted stone mustering ground here, muttering in hushed, somber tones. Before them, atop a weathered wooden platform, paces Field Marshal Kroft, her eyebrows arched sternly as she momentarily tolerates the crowd's murmurs. Behind her upon the scaffold stand three grizzled veteran guards at attention, as well as an ominous-looking group.

These newcomers wear cowled robes of oily-looking leather, supple gloves, and wide black hats. Some grip heavy canes, others dark satchels. Each of them, though, wears a dark-goggled mask tapering to a pointed beak. Among them stand two others. The first is a middle-aged gentleman in a simple black overcoat with streaks of white gracing the sides of his short, dark hair. He watches the gathered guards with a soft, concerned expression, his hands tightly clasping a heavylooking doctor's case.

The second figure is an imposing one: a Gray Maiden, anonymous in her crimson-plumed full plate.

The Field Marshal's fierce tone cuts through the rumble of whispers as she addresses her gathered guards. 'You will escort Doctor Davaulus and his physicians in their royal duties wherever those might take them. Furthermore, you are to consider orders from any of the queen's Gray Maidens to be as binding as those of any superior officer in the Korvosan Guard. You are the Korvosan Guard. You will not balk. These are dire times, and your city needs these healers. Your city needs you. Your patrol leaders have your assignments. Dismissed!'

The grunts gripe quietly afterward — offended that outsiders now outrank them, and afraid the doctors will bring the sickness with them. The armored woman is Kordaitra Destaid, the Gray Maidens' quartermaster and second-in-command; she takes no notice of the PCs now but confronts them later. Kroft (guarded in this company — the doctors unnerve her too) introduces Dr. Reiner Davaulus: calm, fatherly, deliberate. He welcomes questions, defers detailed plans until he has conferred with Kroft, and produces the queen's public writ from his bag — one of hundreds soon posted across the city:

By Decree of Her Royal Majesty, the Radiant Queen Ileosa, all citizens and members of the Korvosan Guard are to aid and admit the newly established Queen's Physicians in this time of urgency. These royal agents will extend healing to those who are ill and organize defense against the spreading affliction known as 'blood veil.' They are to be allowed access to any home or building they deem necessary in the course of their duties. All those suffering from disease or disorder are to submit themselves to the Physicians for treatment.

To aid in the duties of the Queen's Physicians, know that the order of the Gray Maidens shall also provide military support as needed. The Maidens answer directly to the Crimson Throne, and will be called upon as necessary to augment and strengthen the peace where city guards will not suffice.

Impeding or distracting the Queen's Physicians or the Gray Maidens in the pursuit of their duties is punishable by imprisonment. Impersonating one of the Queen's Physicians or Gray Maidens is punishable by death. Knowingly harboring or hiding the infected is punishable by death. Purposefully spreading blood veil is punishable by torture, then death.

The Queen's Physicians will be making rounds of every city district henceforth until Her Majesty deems this misfortune has abated.

(Give the players the decree as a handout — it is the chapter's social contract, and its penalty clauses come back to bite anyone who later impersonates a physician or hides a sick friend.) Questioned directly, the masked physicians say little and defer to Davaulus, voices muffled and disembodied; asked to prove themselves human, one lifts its mask to show an ordinary face — each claims to be a local healer or a country doctor rushed in from Harse, Palin's Cove, or Veldraine. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ishaniDhatri0001]{Ishani} offers to coordinate the Abadaran effort and heads inside, cautiously optimistic; the meeting that follows yields only talk of quarantines and movement limits.

The truth. Davaulus is a Red Mantis assassin — a poisoner who masterminded blood veil with Andaisin and Ramoska; his masked 'physicians' are @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.queensPhysician1]{Urgathoa cultists} who spread the plague while feigning to treat it. Lifting a mask does not remove it — their @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.appPlagueMask001]{plaguebringer's masks} keep veiling their auras and faith. His one tell: he praises the queen too warmly, and he never lies when he doesn't have to. He is the Hospice finale boss (Part 3).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Combating the Plague — p.88.

Kroft (and Davaulus, for show) sets the PCs to investigate the plague's source and stem it. The chapter offers five missions, each opened by its own hook and run in any order over the days the city sickens — every mission completed saves lives (Survivor Count) and adds clues pointing to the Hospice of the Blessed Maiden. Attempting all five isn't required — but jumping the gun on the hospice costs the party the gear, levels, and clues the missions provide, and ends the chapter with far more dead.

The Quarantine of Old Korvosa. After the PCs complete two or three missions (and before the Hospice in Part 3), Ileosa quarantines all of Old Korvosa: the Gray Maidens burn every wooden bridge and barricade the one stone bridge. This cannot be prevented — it sets up Chapter 3. (How to come and go appears in Chapter 3.)

pages.name: Mission 1: The Wreck of the Direption

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Mission 1 — the Direption — p.89.

Cressida gives the wreck's location. Diving the muddy Jeggare, @Check[type:perception|dc:19] locates the ship (@Check[type:perception|dc:23] without a light source); @Check[type:athletics|dc:16] swims the placid deep. Every 10 minutes searching carries a cumulative chance of drawing the silt eels. @Check[type:society|dc:21] identifies a Nidalese merchantman; @Check[type:society|dc:28] (Guard records) traces it to the long-'lost' Direption of Garagori Whenston — a dead-end that is precisely the point.

It is a red herring. The ship carries no plague source — only clues that waste days. Lady Andaisin left a lethal surprise: the sea-hag druid @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.yviccaSeaHag0001]{Yvicca} and her jigsaw-shark companion Skinshear, moved into the wreck to kill investigators. The hull lies split in two over a rock; the stern's main hatch (@Check[type:athletics|dc:24]) and hold trap door (@Check[type:athletics|dc:22]) are swollen shut, but a 10-ft trebuchet hole opens easy access to A2.

This is an underwater dungeon — without water-breathing/freedom of movement the PCs fight at a real disadvantage; flag that to the GM. Re-judge attack/movement penalties per the PF2e aquatic-combat rules.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, A1 — p.90.

The front portion of the ship broke away and landed awkwardly here, revealing two splintered decks inside. Its bowsprit shattered and decks filled with debris, the vessel bears an ominous moniker along its fire-scarred hull: Direption .

The bow emptied its contents into the current as it sank; the forecastle and galley doors swing open in the flow, and @Check[type:perception|dc:20] confirms there were no supplies and no personal goods aboard — the first hint the ship sailed empty. Six @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.siltEel000000001]{silt eels} nest in the scattered pots and overturned bunks, lunging at anything that disturbs them.

  • 6× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.siltEel000000001]{Silt Eel (1)}
Silt eel nestModerate · 90 XP @ L4

6 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.siltEel000000001]{silt eels} swarm — individually weak, but their stacking venom and the underwater footing make them a real nuisance. Wounded eels flee to the bow and there fight to the death.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, A2 — p.90.

A yawning wound in the ship's charred timbers allows murky water to flow through this debris-cluttered hold. Loose timbers, small fish, and dozens of identical boxes float eerily in the quiet darkness.

The hold carried no plague — the ship sailed in essentially empty, crewed by cultists who abandoned it at the river mouth, leaving only the zealot Rois Vindmel (A4) to pilot it in. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.yviccaSeaHag0001]{Yvicca} has set her jigsaw-shark companion @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.RiqACmnzGmuRaDxP]{Skinshear} here to guard the way down. It circles, butts the door to A3 to warn its mistress, then attacks.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.RiqACmnzGmuRaDxP]{Skinshear (3)}
Skinshear (Yvicca's shark)Trivial · 30 XP @ L4

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.RiqACmnzGmuRaDxP]{Skinshear} — Yvicca's unusually large jigsaw shark, statted as his own creature (no Elite adjustment needed). It fights to the death and alerts Yvicca.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, A3 — p.91.

Several hammocks drift in the murky waters, strung between beams in this room. The room swirls with a haze of gore, fish heads, and half-eaten eels chumming the circling waters.

As in the hold, no bodies and no personal effects — the ship had no crew when it went down. The chum is @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.yviccaSeaHag0001]{Yvicca's} housekeeping: the sea-hag druid lairs here. Warned by Skinshear, she prepares (Evil Eye first, then joins the fight in A2); surprised here, she shrieks for her shark on the first round.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.yviccaSeaHag0001]{Yvicca (3)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.RiqACmnzGmuRaDxP]{Skinshear (3)}
BOSS — YviccaTrivial · 30 XP @ L4

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.yviccaSeaHag0001]{Yvicca}, a sea-hag druid (with Skinshear if it still lives). Underwater, at range, with Horrific Appearance and Tanglevine, she is a genuine threat for a party that came unprepared. She fights to the death.

Yvicca's effects (her actual gear — the source places no hoard here): her wand of cure moderate wounds (18 charges; she burns it on herself below 15 HP unless surrounded — as a PF2e wand of heal, 2nd rank) and her +1 shortspear. Nothing else: the emptiness is the clue. No plague source is here.

Clearing the wreck proves the plague's source lies elsewhere — pushing the PCs toward Carowyn Manor / Racker's Alley and, ultimately, the Hospice.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, A4 — p.91.

Along with a few other bits of ruined furnishings, the tattered sheets of a canopied bed twist like ghosts above the snapped wooden bed frame. Knotted amid the linens, a drowned man idly floats in the room's murky waters. A closed footlocker sits on its side at the far end of the room.

The swollen door takes @Check[type:athletics|dc:21] to force. The corpse is Rois Vindmel — the Urgathoan zealot who steered the ship in, knocked out against his own footlocker as she sank. His face is crab-eaten and his tongue gone: speak with dead yields little (@Check[type:perception|dc:21] to make out his tongueless howl of one word — 'Andaisin'). His silver unholy symbol of Urgathoa hangs at his neck.

@Check[type:perception|dc:16] finds skin and hair on the footlocker's corner (the 'murder weapon'); @Check[type:perception|dc:21] confirms its seal held — the contents are dry. Inside: ~90 gp re-scaled in coin, a cloak of resistance +2 (resilient rune; an embroidered Urgathoan symbol comes off with @Check[type:crafting|dc:21] — a failure leaves the cloak broken, +1 until repaired), and Serving Your Hunger, Urgathoa's sacred text — exquisitely bound (~150 gp re-scaled bounty from any non-evil Korvosan church; ruined to a third of that if the footlocker is opened underwater). Rois has scratched out 'Urgathoa' throughout and penned in 'Andaisin.'

The name that matters. @Check[type:society|dc:21] or @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:23] (gather information) — or simply telling Cressida — reveals: years ago the Nidalese village of Beorandy died of engineered plague at the hands of an Urgathoan cell led by Andaisin, still wanted in Pangolais for mass murder. Cressida grows grim: the 'plague ship' was a decoy, and a plague-cult is loose in Korvosa. Story awards: the cult connection (1,600 XP) and Andaisin's significance (1,200 XP).

Survivor Count: the wreck is the conspirators' time-sink — every day past the first spent on the Direption costs 10 citizens from the chapter's saved-lives tally (and feeds the Epidemic Clock).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Mission 2 — Plague Rats — p.92.

Plague-panicked mobs have begun lynching Korvosa's reclusive wererats, and the firebrand Girrigz Ripperclaws answers with a call to war on the city above. An elder wererat, Eries Yelloweyes (50 years hidden as a fishmonger), fears a purge and approaches the PCs in human form: stop Girrigz — ideally without a slaughter of her people — and she'll have her kin fight the plague from the shadows (disposing of infected bodies). She gives directions to his lair beneath Midland.

The sewers are slick: moving faster than half Speed or attacking in the sewage flow forces @Check[type:acrobatics|dc:18] or fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. A @Check[type:nature|dc:19] recognizes the alarm-fungus shrieker in B2.

Survivor Count: scattering Girrigz's warband without killing the other (coerced) wererats saves 400 citizens and seeds an ongoing wererat ally network — a major win for the persistent-plague Epidemic Clock.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, B1 — p.92.

Through the disgustingly visible haze of noxious sewer reek, the flow of unmentionable slop through the sewer tunnel's filth-slick channel unexpectedly forks. Most of the muck continues on its expected path, but a small stream of ooze diverts off through a wide cleft in the moldy masonry wall. The man-sized crack cuts deep into the rock behind the wall, and wisps of thin white smoke issue forth.

The approach to Girrigz's camp through Korvosa's sewers (pad it with wandering-monster encounters if the party needs XP). The main tunnel bends east another 20 feet to a rusty grate overlooking B4.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, B2 — p.92.

The flow of sewer filth oozes into this rough-hewn stone cave from the west, pooling to the south before continuing through a crude channel in the eastern wall. Fat black mushrooms and other disgusting fungus grow thick around the pool of slime. Several low alcoves are cut into the walls, each filled with moldering hay, filthy furs, and tiny bones.

A guard post around a sewage pool. A @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.GoOuyKBGBocKnq7D]{shrieker fungus} by the south wall screeches if any creature enters and doesn't immediately move north past the central pillar — its noise summons the B3 wererats and the B5 swarm, and wakes the B4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.kPBSSOuR8n8vZHXB]{otyugh}.

  • 2× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2k0JX3RFTVRf0KS2]{Wererat (2)}
  • 3× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.iIJPJcDT8wlJ8z5M]{Giant Rat (−1)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.GoOuyKBGBocKnq7D]{Shrieker (Hazard −1)}
Wererat guardsTrivial · 30 XP @ L5

2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2k0JX3RFTVRf0KS2]{wererats} and 3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.iIJPJcDT8wlJ8z5M]{giant rats} (the rats avoid crossing the water lest they trip the shrieker; the wererats cross it deliberately to raise the alarm).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, B3 — p.93.

Several pieces of broken furniture, dried hay, and fragments of lumber burn in a small fire at the center of this open, ruggedly carved cavern. Short alcoves filled with filthy, oversized rats' nests dot the walls, and the disgusting drip of polluted black condensation echoes through the chamber. A thick flow of sewage spreads across the western edge of the chamber, seeping from a crack in the western wall to another in the south.

The wererats' fire-lit warren of nests. The floor is slippery in the sewage flow (as B2). @Check[type:perception|dc:21] finds a squeeze-hole to B6; @Check[type:crafting|dc:19] or @Check[type:perception|dc:21] notes the subsiding south wall — chipping it (Hardness 8, 40 HP) lets the trapped @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.kPBSSOuR8n8vZHXB]{otyugh} rampage through.

  • 4× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2k0JX3RFTVRf0KS2]{Wererat (2)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.girrigzRipper001]{Girrigz (6, reinforces)}
Wererat den (Girrigz reinforces)Low · 60 XP @ L5

4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2k0JX3RFTVRf0KS2]{wererats}. If fighting erupts here, @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.girrigzRipper001]{Girrigz} comes through the east wall from B6 in his dire-rat form.

@Check[type:perception|dc:19]: 3 smokesticks, a tanglefoot bag, a thunderstone, 20 tindertwigs, a copper coat-of-arms trumpet (~15 gp re-scaled), and masterwork carpenter's tools.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, B4 — p.93.

Two thick grates of rusted iron hedge in this section of sewer tunnel. From a human-sized crack in the northern wall seeps a steady flow of sewage, oozing into the greater flow of tainted water. A hulking pile of filth and debris partially blocks the stream of offal, forming a cart-sized clot in this disgusting artery.

The wererats have penned a lone @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.kPBSSOuR8n8vZHXB]{otyugh} between two grates (Hardness 10, 60 HP, lift @Check[type:athletics|dc:26]; the jammed east-grate winch retracts it after a minute's cranking). Starving, it sleeps until noise wakes it.

The otyugh ('so hungeries!') can be turned against the wererats: @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:23] (+4 if fed) wins an hour's help, or @Check[type:intimidation|dc:20] buys 10 minutes — after which it forgets and tries to eat the PCs.

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.kPBSSOuR8n8vZHXB]{Otyugh (4)}
Otyugh (if it attacks)Trivial · 30 XP @ L5

One hungry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.kPBSSOuR8n8vZHXB]{otyugh} — a potential ally or a third combatant in the wererat fight.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, B5 — p.93.

The scrapes and scratches of tiny claws cover the uneven walls of this crudely carved cave. Amid these marks, dozens of dark, fist-sized holes dot the stone like a rocky beehive. Hundreds of bones-of rats, horses, fish, and humans-lie scattered across the floor, and a cleft in the earthen wall leads off to the east.

A bone-littered nest cave. The colony attacks anything that doesn't smell of rodent; the swarm investigates if the shrieker sounds, but the dire rats are too lazy to.

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.6wPW2dvpt86Ou6bL]{Rat Swarm (1)}
  • 6× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.iIJPJcDT8wlJ8z5M]{Giant Rat (−1)}
Rat nestTrivial · 10 XP @ L5

A @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.6wPW2dvpt86Ou6bL]{rat swarm} and 6 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.iIJPJcDT8wlJ8z5M]{giant rats}.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, B6 — p.94.

Several crates, mismatched boxes, and poorly kept weapons lie stacked and scattered about this dingy stone chamber. Pinned to the side of one stack is a crudely sketched map of Korvosa. A large rat's nest, strewn with moldy pillows, fills an elevated hollow in the south wall.

The warlord's command den. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.girrigzRipper001]{Girrigz} plots his war here, dreaming of burning Castle Korvosa — unwarned, he is caught studying his map. He doesn't react to the shrieker, but fighting in B3 or the otyugh's release brings him (pre-buffed, in dire-rat form through the B3 wall-crack).

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.girrigzRipper001]{Girrigz Ripperclaws (6)}
BOSS — Girrigz RipperclawsLow · 60 XP @ L5

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.girrigzRipper001]{Girrigz} — pre-buffed if warned, striking from stealth with his runed rapier and Vital Strike. He cannot be reasoned with and fights to the death.

@Check[type:perception|dc:19]: an arms cache (22 daggers, 12 short swords, 3 crossbows, 4 chain shirts, 12 alchemist's fire, a masterwork longsword + chainmail). @Check[type:perception|dc:21] in the nest: 4 potions of healing, a bottle of air, and a pearl of power (2nd-rank) — plus the squeeze-crack to B3. Girrigz carries ~23 gp re-scaled besides his runed mithral rapier.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Mission 3 — The Color of Death — p.96.

The Lavender perfumery off Summoning Street, owned by the Chelish con-artist @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vendraLoaggri001]{Vendra Loaggri}, is selling 'Lavender's Luxuriant Liniment' — a 2-gp 'plague cure' that is sugar, cheap scent, and river water. By day a queue stretches four blocks; the mission is to expose the fraud, not to fight.

Cracking the con: @Check[type:crafting|dc:22] (an hour with an alchemist's lab) reveals the liniment is worthless; @Check[type:perception|dc:20] spots her planted shill Solt Carmino (the only 'customer' browsing perfume), and @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:24] or @Check[type:intimidation|dc:24] makes him confess. The cleanest proof: administer a dose to a real plague victim before a Korvosan Guard (Grau or Kroft will witness), then actually cure them.

This 'cure' is a dead end for the plague (and a moral test) — it heals no one. Watching Vendra's thugs haul river-water barrels from the Jeggare by night (easy to tail) is the fastest evidence. Report to Cressida and the Guard arrests her.

Story award: expose the scam and shut Lavender down → 1,600 XP, plus full XP for everyone (Vendra included) the PCs didn't have to defeat in combat.

Survivor Count: ending the false cure saves 700 citizens — people who would have contracted and spread blood veil believing the liniment protected them.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, C1 — p.96.

Heady scents twist throughout the cramped but stylish perfumery. A dizzying assortment of bottles-from gaudy ceramic containers to graceful crystalline vials-lines a variety of lace- and ribbon-strewn tables, shelves, and racks, as well as an eye-catching display in the wide front window. Across from the front door's lavender-tinted glass panes runs a long counter, stacked high with hundreds of simple clay phials bearing round, magenta stoppers. Behind the counter, violet flourishes swoop across a sign reading, 'Lavender's Luxuriant Liniment: Either You've Got It, or You've Had It.'

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vendraLoaggri001]{Vendra} works the crowd while two shop-girls sell and two @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lavenderThug0001]{Lavender thugs} watch with saps. Confront her with proof and she screams for the PCs' ejection (offering free doses to win her customers back); cornered, she flees toward C2, knifing a PC on the way out.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vendraLoaggri001]{Vendra Loaggri (6)}
  • 2× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lavenderThug0001]{Lavender Thug (2)}
Vendra + thugs (by day)Low · 60 XP @ L6

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vendraLoaggri001]{Vendra} and 2 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lavenderThug0001]{Lavender thugs} amid scattering customers (CR7-equivalent by day; fewer guards and no crowd at night make it easier). Vendra fights dirty and runs.

@Check[type:crafting|dc:20] picks out genuine perfumes worth ~80 gp re-scaled; the counter box holds ~42 gp in recent sales, and the safe (@Check[type:thievery|dc:26]) ~76 gp in coin + three carved lapis perfume bottles (~5 gp each). Vendra carries a circlet of persuasion, a wand of charm person, a wand of remove disease (3 charges — she's saving the last to flee the city), and ~18 gp of silver-and-violet jewelry.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, C2 — p.99.

Delicate wall hangings, artistically shaped candles, and the fine scent of cherry blossoms fill this well-decorated apartment. A table sculpted with swirling ivy leaves bears a fragile porcelain tea service and an exotically curved hookah in a kitchen nook to the east. A door adjacent to the kitchen opens into a bedroom furnished with an antique armoire and a bed heavily laden with round pillows and draped with purple silk bedding.

A locked (@Check[type:thievery|dc:21]), prim parody of a Chelish noble's salon — tawdry romances, maudlin poetry, amateur art, and no alchemy anywhere. @Check[type:perception|dc:21] finds a corner-hinged secret door into the boarded-up lab (C3). By night there's a 50% chance Vendra sleeps here; otherwise she's mixing brew in C3.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, C3 — p.99.

Bits of broken crates and barrels cover the floor of this dilapidated apartment. A tub of oily liquid, its lip about four feet high, fills a corner of the room, a well-used oar sticking out of it. Next to it squat several large casks of murky water and two stacks of boxes. One holds dozens of small ceramic vials with magenta stoppers; the other holds a mismatched collection of delicate perfume bottles. The apartment's kitchen nook holds another crate, this one filled with broken shards of multicolored glass. Despite being in shambles, the apartment smells delightfully of spices, flowers, and exotic oils.

The 'condemned' apartment behind the shop, boarded shut inside and out (Hardness 5, 15 HP, @Check[type:athletics|dc:24] to break), reachable by the C2 secret door. Here the fake cure is mixed from river water hauled in nightly — the bottles, perfumes, and water barrels are all the hard evidence the Guard needs. By day one @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lavenderThug0001]{thug} works; by night Vendra and two thugs brew.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lavenderThug0001]{Lavender Thug (2)}
Lab guardsTrivial · 10 XP @ L6

1 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lavenderThug0001]{Lavender thug} by day (more at night with Vendra).

The lab equipment functions as an alchemist's lab; the raw ingredients are ~80 gp re-scaled of potion/alchemy reagents — a useful crafting stock for the party.

Mission complete: the perfumery con collapses. It cured nothing — the real source is still out there, pointing the PCs onward (Racker's Alley, Carowyn Manor, the Hospice).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Mission 4 — The Hungry Dead — p.100.

As bodies pile up, lazy plague-carters have been dumping corpses in Racker's Alley (western Old Korvosa) instead of the Gray District. Kroft (or the temple, or rumor) sends the PCs to confirm it and clear any scavengers or undead before guards haul the dead away.

GM: four @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.dFzTXkrpoOOdbzuW]{vampire spawn} — minions of the Ustalavic vampire-count Tiriac, sent to 'assist' (and watch) the nosferatu Ramoska Arkminos — have lairred under Giotorri's Toys and feed on the dumped (and the living). They long ago forgot their charge to watch Ramoska. Ramoska is the Temple-of-Urgathoa villain (Part 3).

Survivor Count: clearing the spawn and confirming the dumping ground (so the Guard can stop it) saves 200 citizens — feed it into the Epidemic Clock.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, D1 — p.100.

The high walls of the surrounding buildings throw this awkwardly bent alley into constant shadow. Although littered with garbage and filth, the refuse isn't the most stomachturning trait of this rundown side way. Heaped against a bent wooden wall rises a pile of more than three dozen plague victims, their faces blistered and flushed, eyes open and staring. The scent of death is overpowered by the reek of rot, suggesting that some of these corpses have been here for days, but even a casual glance reveals that certain bodies seem strangely pale compared to most of the victims who succumb to the sickness.

A shadowed, sunless dead-end alley with 40+ corpses heaped against the east wall. @Check[type:perception|dc:20] notes that many bodies are blood-drained with neck/wrist punctures — and bear no blood-veil sores. Contact with the plague-dead exposes a creature to Blood Veil (@Check[type:fortitude|dc:24]). A 10-ft hole in the east wall opens into the workshop (D2).

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.dFzTXkrpoOOdbzuW]{Vampire Spawn (4)}
Vampire spawn sentryTrivial · 20 XP @ L6

By night one @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.dFzTXkrpoOOdbzuW]{vampire spawn} watches from the 20-ft roof (−2 to spot it from the alley floor). Seeing anyone touch the bodies, it shrieks and attacks; its 3 companions arrive from D2 in 1d3 rounds.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, D2 — p.101.

Dozens of glass eyes-crooked and crazed-glare from the heads of malformed and half-carved dolls lining skewed workroom shelves. Rat-gnawed stuffed aurochs, disembodied doll limbs, miniature rolling elephants, unseaworthy miniature wooden ships, and blocks illustrated with deformed or poorly painted animals fill bins and racks about the room. A cracked wooden door leads to the north, while a rickety trap door breaks the sawdust-covered floor to the east. In one corner lies the drying corpse of an old bald man amid the wood chips, rusty tools, and oily rags of a scored workbench.

Beneath the toy-shop: a 3½-foot dirt crawl space (difficult terrain for Medium creatures) under the trap door, where the four @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.dFzTXkrpoOOdbzuW]{vampire spawn} keep their Ustalavic-soil coffins, watched over by the desiccated corpse of the toymaker Rodolfo Giotorri (eight neck-punctures, @Check[type:perception|dc:19] to notice; @Check[type:perception|dc:15] finds his key ring). By day they sleep here (−10 to their Perception; killing one wakes the rest thrashing and howling). Fight all four here if the alley alarm was raised.

  • 4× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.dFzTXkrpoOOdbzuW]{Vampire Spawn (4)}
BOSS — the four vampire spawnModerate · 80 XP @ L6

All 4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.dFzTXkrpoOOdbzuW]{vampire spawn} — blood drain, dominate gaze, fast healing, and their vampire weaknesses (running water, sunlight). They bicker over prey and split up one-per-PC rather than coordinate. A genuine Severe-leaning fight; bait them toward sunlight or a threshold.

@Check[type:perception|dc:21] in the coffin soil: ~45 gp re-scaled of Ustalavic-minted coin, a ring of jumping (brass spider-legs), and pipes of haunting (bone).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, D3 — p.101.

Awkwardly stitched stuffed animals, poorly equipped toy soldiers, and dolls exhibiting myriad accidental deformities stare blankly out of the filth-smeared front window of this toy store showroom. Several heavy-looking kites dangle purple and crimson tails from the ceiling above, and a dollhouse recreating Castle Korvosa's intimidating towers dominates one of the room's side tables. Festooned with tiny bells, the shop's entrance stands to the north, across from a doorway marked 'Private' and a counter cluttered with dusty jars of candies.

The dusty, locked showroom above (@Check[type:thievery|dc:28], or Giotorri's key from D2). The spawn left the cheap, defective toys alone — which is why nobody ever investigated.

@Check[type:perception|dc:19]: a jingling lockbox (@Check[type:thievery|dc:21]) with a few coins, a silver tooth, and an Abadaran deposit-box key (No. 261; @Check[type:society|dc:20] or @Check[type:religion|dc:20] to place it). Redeemed at the Grand Vault, it yields Giotorri's life savings (~7 gp re-scaled), masterwork thieves' tools, boots of striding and springing, and a sheaf of terrible toy designs.

Clearing Racker's Alley removes one of Ramoska's loose ends — and quietly establishes the vampire's presence ahead of the Temple finale.

pages.name: Mission 5: The Case of the Vanishing Virtuoso

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Mission 5 — Carowyn Manor — p.104.

Deyanira Mirukova begs the PCs (or Kroft refers her) to find her brother Ruan, a Varisian ocarina prodigy who went to perform at a masquerade at Carowyn Manor and never returned. The estate now sits locked, curtained, and reeking. @Check[type:society|dc:21] recognizes Ruan as a genuine virtuoso. (Her reward is meager — heirlooms under 100 gp and Marbledome passes — but the trail matters.)

GM: Ileosa added rich undesirables to the plague's toll; Davaulus farmed the hit-list to the necromancer Rolth Lamm (Gaedren's son, the absent master of the Ch.1 Dead Warrens), who — absorbed in studying Varisian plague-immunity — handed it to his lover @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.jolistinaSusp001]{Jolistina Susperio}. She murdered the entire party, raised 21 of the guests as zombies, and disguised every corpse as a blood-veil death. The 'plague' here is staged.

Getting in: the mahogany front doors are locked (@Check[type:athletics|dc:26] / @Check[type:thievery|dc:29]); the iron fence takes @Check[type:athletics|dc:20] to Climb; the balcony banisters take @Check[type:athletics|dc:21] to climb; the spiked garden hedge @Check[type:athletics|dc:19] (fail by 5+ → @Damage[1d6[piercing]]). Jolistina jammed the side doors and shutters (@Check[type:athletics|dc:25]/@Check[type:thievery|dc:24] doors; windows @Check[type:thievery|dc:24]).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, E1 — p.106.

A massacre took place here. Upon the marble floor and heaped in the corners lie more than a dozen corpses, each clad in garish outfits of sequined velvet, revealing silk, and colorful feathers. Masks of all shapes and sizes-each competing with the last in terms of elaborateness-adorn the dead. In several cases, though, these fanciful adornments have fallen away, revealing withered flesh covered in the telltale blisters of blood veil. Most horrifyingly, upon a bloodslick space cleared at the room's center sway three couples, jerking like hellish dancers, all obviously dead.

The whole manor is one complex encounter. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.jolistinaSusp001]{Jolistina} hides and plays cat-and-mouse, ordering her posed zombies to defend her while she snipes and Vanishes. Of her 21 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{zombies}, 7 are uncontrolled (they attack anyone — Jolistina included; she avoids those rooms). Every corpse, dead or undead, was disguised with sculpt corpse to look like a blood-veil death: @Check[type:medicine|dc:20] (or @Check[type:will|dc:11]) while examining a body reveals the symptoms are manufactured, and none are contagious.

  • 6× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{Human Zombie (−1)}
E1a Great Hall — dancing deadTrivial · 0 XP @ L6

6 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{human zombies} posed as dancing noble couples in the great hall (the read-aloud above); they turn and attack 1 round after the PCs enter.

  • 3× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{Human Zombie (−1)}
E1b Den — uncontrolled trioTrivial · 0 XP @ L6

3 uncontrolled @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{zombies}: a lion- and lioness-masked couple seated before the cold fireplace, attended by a peacock-costumed servant with a silver tray. They attack the moment anyone enters.

  • 4× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{Human Zombie (−1)}
E1c Dining Room — dinersTrivial · 0 XP @ L6

Eight corpses sit at a finely set table; 4 are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{zombies} mechanically spooning rotten food into slack jaws. They attack on entry but must each spend an action extricating themselves from their pushed-in chairs.

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{Human Zombie (−1)}
E1d Recital Hall — the harpistTrivial · 0 XP @ L6

1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{zombie} dressed as a blue-winged angel strums a standing harp whose every string is broken, before an audience of four costumed corpses (a sea serpent, a castle tower, a swan, a blue skeleton). It attacks on entry.

  • 2× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{Human Zombie (−1)}
E1e Kitchen — the carversTrivial · 0 XP @ L6

2 uncontrolled @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{zombies} — the Carowyns' servants — sit with dull knives, pantomiming the carving of a corpse dressed as a huge pig. They attack on entry.

pages.name: E2. Carowyn Manor — Second Floor & Cellar

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, E2 — p.108.

Jolistina's remaining 5 posed @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{zombies} haunt the upper floor. Somewhere below, the surviving host Ausio Carowyn hides — and the gallery holds the mission's crucial absence.

  • 3× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{Human Zombie (−1)}
E2a Gallery — the criticsTrivial · 0 XP @ L6

3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{zombies} in flashy metallic costumes wander the picture gallery, pantomiming appreciation and criticism of the Korvosan cityscapes and Carowyn portraits, stumbling over the dozen corpses posed as ghastly statues and contemplative critics. They attack on entry. The clue: a search turns up the bodies of three musicians — Ruan's corpse is not among them.

  • 2× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{Human Zombie (−1)}
E2b Bedroom — the Galtan queenTrivial · 0 XP @ L6

2 uncontrolled @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{zombies}: Olauren Carowyn in a pearl-studded gown and ceiling-brushing powdered wig, attended by a 'Qadiran princess.' They attack 1 round after entry. (What the PCs do with Olauren matters to Ausio below — see his entry.)

The wine cellar.

A well-stocked wine rack and several large casks line the walls of this stuffy cellar. A small wooden door squats in the southeastern corner.

Behind the small door is the converted studio of @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch2AusioCarowyn1]{Ausio Carowyn} — locked from within (@Check[type:thievery|dc:29] to bypass or @Check[type:athletics|dc:24] to batter down). Inside, among painting supplies: a tawdry divan, a small shrine to Shelyn, several scandalous portraits of Olauren — and Ausio himself, shaking, brandishing a dull paint knife. He fled at the first bolt and has lived for days on a water cask and caught rats, listening to shuffling feet and shrill laughter above. Once he believes the PCs mean no harm, he gives what he saw:

'We were greeting the Westerkiers when the elven acrobat we hired as an entertainer went crazy. I saw her open fire on one of my guests with her crossbow, laughing all the while in the most frightful, shrill way. I've been locked in here for days, and every time I got up the courage to sneak out, I heard that horrible laughter above. Is she dead? Is it safe?'

He asks after his wife: he expected her death, but learning she was made undead — or being shown her body, mangled or worse still walking — breaks his already traumatized mind. Asked about Ruan, he last saw the youth in the second-floor gallery.

Story award: rescue Ausio → XP as if he'd been defeated in combat (he is not Jolistina's plaything — she never found him).

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.jolistinaSusp001]{Jolistina Susperio (8)}
  • + remaining @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{zombies}
BOSS — Jolistina SusperioModerate · 80 XP @ L6

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.jolistinaSusp001]{Jolistina} makes her stand (or her endless retreat) here, fighting from behind whatever zombies remain. Run her as the manor-spanning skirmish she is, not a stand-up fight. She surrenders at ≤10 HP, spilling everything (→ What Jolistina Knows); if she escapes instead, she seeks out Rolth — the PCs may meet her again at his side in the temple.

Jolistina carries 2 potions of invisibility, a potion of remove disease, wands of animate dead / sculpt corpse / cat's grace, +1 glamered studded leather, a hand crossbow with 3 screaming bolts, 4 alchemist's fire, 4 smoke bombs, 4 doses of pesh, and ~23 gp re-scaled in coin and stolen jewelry. The manor itself holds a noble household's worth of re-scaled valuables.

The reveal: exposing the staged deaths proves the nobles were murdered, not plagued — and the wand of sculpt corpse + Jolistina's link to Rolth ties the killings to the necromancer and the cult of Urgathoa, pointing toward the Hospice. Save Ausio. Ruan is not dead — he was carried off alive (see below) and can be rescued from Ramoska's laboratory (G11).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, What Jolistina Knows / Ruan's Fate — p.109.

Captured alive, @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.jolistinaSusp001]{Jolistina} knows much — but she is thoroughly insane. Unless charmed or magically compelled, getting her to talk takes @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:33]. Whatever the result she giggles that the speaker's tone reminds her of 'my darling Rolth's silken tongue.'

Intimidation is a trap with one lever: the forlorn elf is a fatalistic masochist — threats of pain, torture, even infection with blood veil delight her (expect exaggerated scandalous cries). She is immune to all of it except threats of imprisonment or of exposing her failure to Rolth: with that lever, @Check[type:intimidation|dc:22] gets her talking.

Her testimony: Rolth sent her to kill several 'rich bastards' due at the party; everyone else was a bonus. She doesn't know why — she suspects his new employer wanted it, since Rolth knows no such people — nor who the employer is, only that he (or she — an infuriating possibility) keeps Rolth holed up in a hidden laboratory somewhere in the city. The plague disguise? 'Another of my darling Rolth's ideas, but a good one.' On Ruan: Rolth asked her to take alive any young, healthy Varisians; Ruan was the only one. She trussed him up, and the morning after the massacre Rolth collected him — with two of those 'bird-masked doctors' at his side, who carried the youth off to who-knows-where. Released, she finds a pesh den and pines; she asks the PCs to tell Rolth she misses him.

Redemption (optional): supportive PCs who take the mad elf in hand — and weather her violent outbursts — can, at your discretion, slowly mend her; redeemed, Jolistina makes a memorable ally or follower.

Story award: learn what Jolistina knows → 2,400 XP (besides combat XP). Survivor Count: destroying the manor's zombies — and stopping Jolistina before boredom sent her creations into the streets, or her on to other manors — saves 500 citizens.

Ruan's fate. Good news and bad for Deyanira: her brother was not among the dead — and a necromancer's masked accomplices carried him off somewhere unknown. The news does little to ease her. She pays what she promised, begs the PCs to chase any rumor of him, and excuses herself, distraught. Ruan is alive — strapped to Ramoska Arkminos's operating table in the Temple of Urgathoa (G11), where the PCs can still save him in Part 3.

pages.name: Part 3 — Epidemic: The Hospice of the Blessed Maiden

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Part 3: Epidemic / F. Hospice — p.110.

Part 3 — Epidemic. The trail converges on the Hospice of the Blessed Maiden, the Queen's Physicians' headquarters in a converted warehouse — and on the hidden Temple of Urgathoa beneath it. The PCs should be 7th level entering the temple, and just 8th by the chapter's end. This is the plague's heart: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.reinerDavaulus01]{Davaulus}, @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.andaisinUrgath01]{Andaisin}, and the nosferatu @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ramoskaArkminos1]{Ramoska}.

Finding the hospice: Ishani or Cressida simply tell the PCs where it is if asked to investigate the Queen's Physicians (the group makes no secret of its base). Otherwise: @Check[type:society|dc:21] recalls the location, @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:23] gathers it, or the PCs can tail a group of physicians for 1d4 hours (a Stealth check against a physician's Perception DC each hour) back to the building.

The building (GM). Four months ago the Red Mantis pried this West Dock warehouse — one of the Arkonas' most secret smuggling dens — from the family through bribes and blackmail; when the Arkonas tried to leak its hidden lower level to the Guard, the information and everyone who saw it disappeared. Andaisin's cult rebuilt the smuggler's den into a temple. The 'hospice' above is the perfect cover: nobody wants to enter the city's greatest concentration of sickness — and patients admitted here do not come back out; they 'succumb' quickly and the bodies go below. A few days' stake-out makes that arithmetic obvious and damning.

Getting in: the front door is unlocked by day, locked after dark (@Check[type:thievery|dc:29]; Hardness 5, HP 15, @Check[type:athletics|dc:18] to break); the boarded-up rear loading doors are sturdier (Hardness 5, HP 30, @Check[type:athletics|dc:25] — and smashing them can be heard from F2). Bluffing or sneaking beats fighting through; pushing too hard gets the PCs an audience with Davaulus (F6), who tries to misdirect them onto a fictional Ghlaunder-worshiping wererat cult. The city can spare no reinforcements — this is the PCs' job alone (a token squad of guards or acolytes is your call).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, F1 — p.112.

The stinging scent of alcohol and medicine floods this dingy reception room, an odor typical of hospices-and morgues. Across from the entrance sits a long wooden desk, beyond which a stained leather curtain covers an archway, muffling the moans issuing from the rooms beyond.

Six poor citizens — all showing blood-veil symptoms, some advanced — huddle here, some having waited hours. At the desk sits the burly 'nurse' @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch2Bhrunlida0001]{Bhrunlida Torthus}: a hired charlatan (no cultist; she knows nothing of what's below), triple-scarved and heavy-gloved against infection, with no compassion to spare. She takes names, enforces the queue, and shrilly bars anyone heading for the curtain to F2.

@Check[type:diplomacy|dc:25] or @Check[type:intimidation|dc:21] convinces her to fetch Dr. Davaulus: she steps into F2, and after a few minutes the PCs are led through the sick ward and up to F6, where Davaulus waits with the F6 Gray Maidens and the F7 physicians. Forcing past her instead means her bellows alert F2 while she attacks — impatient and eager for the excuse.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch2Bhrunlida0001]{Bhrunlida Torthus (3)}
The nurse (only if forced)Trivial · 10 XP @ L7

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch2Bhrunlida0001]{Bhrunlida Torthus} swings to eject intruders while F2 readies a defense; the waiting patients panic and flee. Below 15 HP she runs or begs; all she can betray is the F2 headcount.

Story award: secure an audience with Davaulus without attacking Bhrunlida → XP as if she'd been defeated in combat.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, F2 — p.113.

The warehouse's vast interior has been converted into one gigantic convalescent ward. The stench of alcohol, sickness, and waste chokes the breath, and tight rows of low, stained cots cram the stone-floored hall. Every bed is filled with a pitiful story-men and women of all walks groaning and wheezing as they're consumed by blood veil, their sufferings multiplied by the echoing chamber.

More than 60 cots, each holding a helpless citizen too weak to rise; the cult's masked @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.queensPhysician1]{physicians} coo insincerely over the dying. A makeshift kitchen fills the northeast (watery gruel); the ceiling rises ~30 feet, with the F5 catwalks spanning at 20. Each round a PC spends in this room, 10% chance of blood-veil exposure — 100% on touching any patient (@Check[type:fortitude|dc:24] as usual).

Keeping cover: unescorted PCs are loudly told to wait their turn in F1; demands to see Davaulus get them parked here 1d4+2 minutes, then invited up to F6. The physicians do not start a fight — they attack only if attacked, if the PCs name the cult of Urgathoa, or if anyone tries to enter the lift room (F4). The Gray Maidens intercept anyone forcing through and have no such compunctions.

  • 2× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenFoot01]{Gray Maiden (3)}
  • 4× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.queensPhysician1]{Queen's Physician (2)}
Ward watchTrivial · 20 XP @ L7

2 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenFoot01]{Gray Maidens} (door to F4 and the stairs; 2 more on the F5 catwalks above ready bows) and 4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.queensPhysician1]{Queen's Physicians} among the cots.

Fighting among the helpless: every bed holds an innocent (helpless, 2 HP). The cult doesn't care; the PCs should. Give any missed ranged attack a 25% chance of striking a patient.

Development — the evacuation. Saving 60 bedridden patients is beyond on-the-spot healing: they need somewhere safe. A PC with standing at a city temple can place 30 there; Cressida Kroft can house 30 in unused guard buildings. If the PCs tip their hand and leave without destroying the cult below, they return to a shuttered hospice and a cover story about 'thieves and malcontents' forcing a relocation — in truth the patients were murdered and delivered below (bolster the temple with extra zombies). Story award: get help for all the patients → 3,200 XP. Survivor Count: every patient who survives the PCs' visit and is cured counts as one life saved.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, F3 — p.114.

The scent of exotic wood and sawdust fills this maze of barrels and crates. A web of ropes and pulleys crisscrosses the ceiling; from one thick cable hangs a net of barrels suspended nearly twenty feet above the splinter-scattered floor below.

Three great wooden doors stand in the northern wall, while leather flaps lead to the south and east.

The remains of the legitimate Arkona import business — most containers empty, their textiles long since moved. The southwest corner holds empty animal cages stenciled with their exotic former occupants ('Three Breaths Viper — Mwangi Expanse,' 'Emperor Cobra (Eggs) — Vudra,' 'Toad Shrew — uncharted island'). Smashing in the alley doors can be heard from F2 (distance-penalized Perception); both F2 Gray Maidens come to investigate anything heard.

A thorough half-hour search and @Check[type:perception|dc:23]: a crate of elegant Vudrani silks — robes, saris, stoles, turbans — worth ~30 gp re-scaled. One sari is bloodstained and slashed: quiet evidence that not everything that happens in this building is medicine.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, F4 — p.114.

Several cubbies and wooden lockers line the walls of this small workroom. Four rickety chairs surround a water-damaged table, and a well-used but solid-looking cargo lift rises up through the ceiling.

The Red Mantis chose this warehouse for this room: the cargo lift is the cleverly concealed — and only — access to the hidden level below. Its controls plainly run the lift up to the floors above, but the operating lever radiates moderate necromancy (a tell for any caster who looks). @Check[type:perception|dc:23] finds a second control set into the lever's slot: a simple socket missing its button. With the button-key seated and pressed, the lift descends to G1 in the Temple of Urgathoa; @Check[type:thievery|dc:33] can force the mechanism to lower without it. The missing button is carried by Dr. Davaulus (F8).

Story award: activate the lift and reach the hidden level → 800 XP.

The temple guards (G2) listen for the lift: descending it loudly is an announced arrival. Note for mapping: the temple lies 40 feet below the warehouse floor.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, F5 — p.114.

A track of sturdy-looking catwalks soar over the warehouse floor below, the evenly placed and well-trod platforms supported from above by iron poles.

Relics of the warehouse's shelving days, 20 feet above the F2 floor. Two @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenFoot01]{Gray Maidens} patrol here: bows down into the sick ward if a fight breaks out below, intercepting on the stairs (or falling back to guard F6) if intruders head up.

  • 2× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenFoot01]{Gray Maiden (3)}
Catwalk patrolTrivial · 20 XP @ L7

2 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenFoot01]{Gray Maidens} — sword-and-shield in the choke of the catwalk, longbows if the PCs hang back.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, F6 — p.115.

The rough functionality of the warehouse below gives way to beige tile and a whitewashed hall on this floor. Double doors engraved with images of rampant gazelles stands to the south, their once fine teak bearing obvious scores and chips from rough use.

The old clerks' entry, now a guardroom. The doors to F7 are kept locked (@Check[type:thievery|dc:29] or @Check[type:athletics|dc:26]; Davaulus holds the key and personally opens them at shift change, dawn and dusk).

  • 2× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenFoot01]{Gray Maiden (3)}
Hall guardsTrivial · 20 XP @ L7

2 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenFoot01]{Gray Maidens} order intruders out — including anyone dressed as staff, since nobody visits outside shift change. Realizing the PCs don't belong, they attack at once, shouting the alarm to F7.

Limits of the disguise ruse: passing as the next shift takes a successful Deception and a disguise (Impersonate) check — and fails automatically if more than 2 PCs pose as Gray Maidens or more than 3 as Queen's Physicians (that's the whole shift). Timing it to dawn/dusk is the only window when staff are expected here at all.

The meeting. If the PCs arranged an audience (via @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch2Bhrunlida0001]{Bhrunlida} or by being pushy), @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.reinerDavaulus01]{Davaulus} receives them here, flanked by the F7 physicians and these guards — see F8 for how he plays it. If combat erupts in this room, the F7 physicians alert the doctor and join within moments.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, F7 — p.115.

Rows of white-sheeted beds line the walls of this room. Each bed holds a patient restrained by leather straps that bind the figure to the sturdy metal frame. At the room's center stretch simple wooden worktables, each covered in fluidfilled beakers, intricate glass tubes, small burners, and other alchemical instruments.

Here the cult's real work shows: 15 Varisian prisoners, drugged unconscious and strapped down, each inexplicably immune to blood veil — and each being examined, operated on, and mutilated to find out why. All are stable but at death's door; even healed, most are too weak from surgeries to move unaided. None know anything of where they are. The tables hold the cult's research notes.

  • 3× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.queensPhysician1]{Queen's Physician (2)}
Physicians at workTrivial · 0 XP @ L7

3 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.queensPhysician1]{Queen's Physicians}, flanking in silence for Sneak Attacks.

The notes — and the alarm. The research notes spell out why Varisians are being studied, and reference the church of Urgathoa — including several reports 'sent below to A.' If the PCs attack the hospice openly, the physicians here burn the notes (a hasty job — surviving scraps still reveal the cult is active below) and cut the throats of the 15 Varisians before joining the defense. Hitting this room fast, or quietly, is the difference between rescue and massacre.

Story award: rescue at least half the prisoners → 1,200 XP. Survivor Count: every Varisian rescued counts as one life saved.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, F8 — p.116.

Blood, bile, and other humors bubble away within oddly formed beakers and twisted tubes in this sizable laboratory. Any wall space left in view between cabinets or over-laden bookshelves is covered by worn parchments depicting magnified aspects of human anatomy in grisly detail-many pierced with pins and flags like the war maps of a veteran campaigner. In the corner, a desk of elegantly carved white ash bears the image of a herd of antelope, but stains from dark chemicals and gore disfigure the once-beautiful piece.

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.reinerDavaulus01]{Dr. Reiner Davaulus}'s laboratory-office. There is no stair here — his escape route is the F4 lift, whose button-key he carries. Confronted by appointment (in F6) he plays the gracious, fatherly host with one goal: send the PCs away — ideally to Old Korvosa chasing his fictional Ghlaunder-worshiping wererat cabal, even hiring them for the errand. PCs who bite walk into a Red Mantis stalking (Chapter 3) while he relocates every incriminating note to the temple's inner sanctum (G14).

Caught here unprepared, he turns angry and imperious: they are interrupting royal work on the cure — high treason — and if that fails he lectures, feigning superiority: disease is the world's way of restoring balance; society's dead weight must periodically be pruned; Korvosa will come out of these dark days stronger. The instant he senses an attack coming, he casts invisibility and runs for the lift — possibly tipping off pursuing PCs to the existence of the level below. If he escapes, he is found again in G5, at Rolth's side.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.reinerDavaulus01]{Reiner Davaulus (9)}
Reiner DavaulusModerate · 80 XP @ L7
