@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.reinerDavaulus01]{Davaulus} (bane-rapier Sneak Attacks, Inspire) — usually backed by the F6/F7 staff if alarmed. He flees to the Temple rather than die in the open; loyal to the Red Mantis, he never surrenders.

@Check[type:perception|dc:21] while searching the desk: Davaulus's scattered notes on the Varisians' immunity — one of the three note-sets behind the cure (→ Chapter Conclusion: Curing Blood Veil); recovering them is worth 2,400 XP. His gear includes ~58 gp re-scaled, the lift button-key, the G3 cabinet and F7 door keys — and a flask of curses neatly labeled 'elixir of true healing', left where a looter would grab it first (@Check[type:will|dc:22] for the drinker).

His notes (here, or relocated to G14 if the PCs were fooled) confirm the manufactured plague. The lift down (F4) begins the Temple of Urgathoa.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, G. Temple of Urgathoa / The Temple Under Alert — p.118.

G. The Temple of Urgathoa — the cult's hidden warren 40 feet beneath the hospice, reached only by the F4 lift. A former smuggler's den, freshly excavated and re-walled in plaster (already water-stained), its murals celebrate plague-drowned cities. Most areas are lit; G1 and G9–G12 are dark. Doors are strong wood (Hardness 5, HP 20, @Check[type:athletics|dc:25] / @Check[type:thievery|dc:29]), locked only where noted.

The Temple Under Alert. The areas below assume the cult doesn't know it's under attack. Once the alarm goes up (Davaulus fleeing down, the G1 doors' linked alarm, or any survivor of a first skirmish):

  • Cultists (14 active): 6 to hold G8, 8 to defend G13.
  • Queen's Physicians (5 active): to the G8 floor.
  • Davaulus: warns Andaisin, then joins G8. Rolth: to the G8 catwalk.
  • Gray Maidens (any who followed Davaulus down): bows trained on G1's doors.
  • Skeletons (G7) and zombies (G9): posted to guard G4.
  • Ramoska: ignores the alarm entirely and keeps working in G11. Andaisin: see G14.

No reinforcements come — blood veil has mostly run its course and the conspirators above are moving on; the fanatics dig in instead (Andaisin animates the cult's dead as new zombies). The PCs can mount multiple assaults: after a week Davaulus and Rolth abandon the cult; 2–3 weeks later Andaisin leads a ritual of self-sacrifice, burns her notes as the last cultist dies, and leaves (perhaps for Deathhead Vault, to face the PCs in Chapter 4).

Staffed by zealous @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.urgathoaCultist1]{cultists of Urgathoa} (and the negotiable nosferatu @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ramoskaArkminos1]{Ramoska} in his lab). Stealth and speed matter — a roused temple masses at its choke points, and the G13 leukodaemon can be turned into anyone's problem.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, G1 — p.119.

The scuffed stone walls of this chamber have been plastered over and then decorated with lurid murals of skeletons cavorting among the dead of a Korvosa that has completely succumbed to blood veil. Simple wooden doors lead to the north, south, and west, each bearing a painting of a scythewielding skeleton. A sizable double door stands on the east wall, appearing in the mural as a massive set of double doors opening into the pyramid foundation of Castle Korvosa. Two more scythe-wielding skeletons decorate these large doors.

The lift from F4 descends into this dark chamber — the temple's threshold. Chapter Conclusion. The Hospice and Temple fall, Davaulus and Andaisin are dead (or fled), and the plague's unnaturally swift, random spread ends — though the disease itself remains at large until cured or burned out. The PCs should be 8th level. The victory is bittersweet — the city is scarred, the Gray Maidens entrenched, and Old Korvosa sealed under quarantine.

A Conspiracy Revealed. Word that the Queen's Physicians were corrupt spreads fast — dangerous knowledge with no hard evidence behind it. The crown's official line: the charming doctor duped Her Majesty; the hospice's Gray Maidens claim ignorance of the chambers below; and if Davaulus survived the PCs, he is publicly executed (or branded a traitor in absentia). Lacking proof, most Korvosans can't believe their queen — vain and unpleasant as she is — capable of such evil; they're simply grateful the scourge has passed, and the city is too wounded to contemplate rebellion. But unrest grows: rumors of royal involvement keep spreading — and so do the queen's resources, with Gray Maidens thicker on every street and the laws turning oppressive. The PCs keep their allies (Cressida, Ishani, Grau, Vencarlo, Tayce), yet should feel distinctly less welcome by the week: Ileosa now knows, beyond doubt, that they are her enemies.

Into Chapter 3. The crafted cure suppresses rather than eradicates (persistent-plague overlay): set the Epidemic Clock from the Final Survivor Count tally. The quarantine of Old Korvosa and the captured seneschal Neolandus (held by the Arkonas) launch 'Escape from Old Korvosa.' If the PCs rode off after Davaulus's fictional wererat cabal, the Red Mantis now hunt them there.

Threads forward: the persistent plague + Epidemic Clock · the Gray Maidens (now a standing enemy) · Rolth's vendetta and his immunity research · Ramoska loose with his bargain (or destroyed at his coffin) · Zenobia the penanggalen (Ch.4) · Trinia & Vencarlo · the Arkonas + Neolandus → Chapter 3.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Curing Blood Veil — p.133.

The cure lies in the research of three demented minds — Davaulus (F8), Rolth (G5), and Ramoska (G11) — each of whom independently found that ~1 in 20 Varisians is immune, none of whom deduced why. With at least one note-set in hand, an alchemist or canny caster can engineer the cure:

  • 1 note-set: @Check[type:crafting|dc:30] and 1 week of work.
  • 2 note-sets: @Check[type:crafting|dc:25] and 1d4+1 days.
  • All 3: @Check[type:crafting|dc:20] and just 1 day.

Brewing doses: with alchemical crafting (or Brew Potion-style magic), ~4 doses per day at ~20 gp re-scaled per day of work, or single doses at @Check[type:crafting|dc:20] for ~10 gp each.

The mercy in its design: the disease's supernatural origin is its undoing — for 1 week after taking the cure, a cured person spreads the cure by contact exactly as blood veil itself spread. Combined with continued lesser restoration and remove disease work, blood veil loses ground daily and is defeated within 1d4+1 weeks.

The Kasanda secret (GM only — hold it back). The original strain, Vorel's phage, was infused with spiritual energy: in 4644 AR, Vorel Foxglove botched his lich apotheosis when his wife Kasanda disrupted the final ritual, and a fragment of her spirit suffused the resulting tainted fungus. The immune Varisians are, in effect, being saved by Kasanda herself — her influence is the flaw that makes a cure possible at all. (Had the three researchers ever compared notes, they'd have engineered the flaw out.) Reveal this only to divination / commune / legend lore-grade magic; it is flavor for connoisseurs of Foxglove family horror, not a required clue.

Survivor Count — cure timing: a cure within a month saves 100 citizens; within a week, 500; within a day, 1,000.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Final Survivor Count — p.134.

After Andaisin's defeat the plague slows but doesn't halt: 1d6 citizens die per day until the cure lands or the disease burns out months later (worst case: 5,000 dead). When one of those endpoints arrives, total the lives saved minus those killed or allowed to die. The chapter's inputs:

The rewards below are cumulative (save 2,801+, get all four); only the XP awards aren't.

  • 700 or fewer — Family Friends: Tayce Soldado organizes the settled Varisians' thanks: a feast at her home and modest gifts — embroidered scarves, jewelry, illustrated harrow decks, fine clothing — totaling ~25 gp re-scaled per PC. 2,400 XP.
  • 701–1,900 — Good Neighbors: dozens of families recognize the PCs; each PC gains a favor from a skilled artisan or artist — a gift worth up to ~100 gp re-scaled, a safe place to lie low for a night, research help (+1 circumstance bonus to the next five relevant Lore/Recall Knowledge checks with the helper's aid), or similar. 3,200 XP.
  • 1,901–2,800 — Local Heroes: Kroft personally thanks the PCs, finally and officially deputizes them into the Korvosan Guard, and opens Citadel Volshyenek's armory: each PC selects gear worth up to ~250 gp re-scaled (magic arms and armor included). 4,800 XP. At this tier and above the PCs have Queen Ileosa's full attention — chance brushes with Gray Maidens and Red Mantis agents may rise.
  • 2,801 or more — City Saviors: the whole city knows them: +1 circumstance bonus to Deception, Diplomacy, and Intimidation against Korvosan citizens. Ileosa is forced to publicly honor them — a short, grudging speech (@Check[type:perception|dc:20] reads how much it costs her) and a writ worth ~500 gp re-scaled per PC, good for any purchase within the walls. (If they've been loud about her involvement, Sabina presents the award instead, the queen pleading exhaustion.) 6,400 XP.

Feed the final tally into the persistent-plague overlay: the Survivor Count sets the Epidemic Clock's starting state for Chapter 3 (Conversion Guide → Blood Veil & the Epidemic Clock).
